mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 150k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rise in housing starts (HOUST) indicates accommodative monetary policy and strong demand, creating positive SPX returns over 60 trading days via improved risk appetite.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns in scenarios with CPIAUCSL < 2.5% and HOUST > 900k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.5 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which drives S&P 500 returns via improved corporate margins and economic growth expectations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 105.5 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 1.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals weakening economic demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDPC1 < 2% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC > -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.7% signals labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer confidence and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in UNRATE > 5.7% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent drop in real sales (RSAFS) and rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates stagflationary pressures, which drive S&P 500 returns lower over 20 trading days via reduced purchasing power and higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in RSAFS < 440k and CPIAUCSL > 2.5% scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or IC > -0.03.
